scope: src/config TypeScript surface after VR/Hub config Rust artifact closeout
generatedFrom: git ls-files src/config '*.ts' '*.d.ts' plus source import audit

classificationContract.native_shell: TypeScript only forwards to Rust/native owners and validates returned shape.
classificationContract.host_io_shell: TypeScript owns Node host IO, injected filesystem, process env publication, cache, or public async/sync entrypoints only.
classificationContract.type_shell: TypeScript declaration/type surface only; no runtime semantic owner.
classificationContract.removable: No source/test/package/docs-live references remain and file can be physically deleted in this slice.
classificationContract.hardStopRule: Every tracked src/config .ts/.d.ts file must be listed with removableNow, classification, ownerFeature, and cannotShrinkFurtherBecause.

entries.minimumTsRole: AuthFile resolver class, in-process key cache, default auth dir host binding, and native key resolution call.
entries.cannotShrinkFurtherBecause: The remaining behavior is host object lifecycle and in-process cache/public class API. Literal removal requires replacing AuthFileResolver consumers with a Rust-backed cache/lifecycle owner or generated host API.
CLI/server/admin callers import this stable config path API. Deletion requires migrating all callers to the native bridge or generated config path facade in one public API migration.

entries.minimumTsRole: Single-file provider config read API, injected sync fs test boundary, TOML-only native detect/decode/coerce calls, and return types.
entries.cannotShrinkFurtherBecause: Production/admin/tests still call the public codec API and sync tests pass injected fsImpl. Removing it requires moving those host IO and injected-fs boundaries to a Rust-backed or generated API.

entries.minimumTsRole: Provider config async native writer facade plus sync injected-fs init writer boundary.
entries.cannotShrinkFurtherBecause: Async writes are Rust-owned, but writeProviderConfigFileSync(fsImpl) is still used by init authoring/tests with an injected synchronous filesystem. Deletion requires removing that injected fs boundary.

entries.minimumTsRole: Provider root selection shell and native root loader call.
entries.cannotShrinkFurtherBecause: Config materialization/admin/CLI callers still import loadProviderConfigsV2 as the public host API. Removing it requires migrating all callers to native/generated provider-root loader bindings.

entries.minimumTsRole: Public loadRouteCodexConfig host-env shell and compatibility export for config source validation.
entries.cannotShrinkFurtherBecause: CLI/server/admin callers import loadRouteCodexConfig. Deletion requires migrating the public config loader API to native/generated bindings and replacing the compatibility export.

entries.minimumTsRole: parseTomlRecord/serializeTomlRecord public wrappers over Rust TOML codec.
entries.cannotShrinkFurtherBecause: CLI/tests/config authoring code import these public helpers. Deletion requires replacing callers with native/generated codec bindings.

entries.minimumTsRole: Public wrapper for Rust comment-preserving scalar patch.
entries.cannotShrinkFurtherBecause: Config writer tests and direct patch callers still import this stable helper. Deletion requires moving those callers to native/generated patch bindings.

entries.minimumTsRole: UnifiedConfigPathResolver class facade over Rust config path resolver.
entries.cannotShrinkFurtherBecause: config-paths.ts and tests still use the public class facade. Deletion requires public path API migration.

entries.minimumTsRole: User config read API, injected sync fs test boundary, TOML-only native detect/decode calls, and return types.
entries.cannotShrinkFurtherBecause: CLI/admin/tests still call the public codec API and sync tests pass injected fsImpl. Removing it requires moving host IO and injected-fs boundaries to a Rust-backed or generated API.

entries.minimumTsRole: Lower-level native manifest/materialization shell and runtime manifest type surface for direct materialization callers.
entries.cannotShrinkFurtherBecause: Server bootstrap/runtime setup and config tests call compileRouteCodexRuntimeConfigManifest/materializeRouteCodexConfig directly. Deletion requires moving these public entrypoints and TypeScript manifest type surface to generated/native bindings.

entries.minimumTsRole: Public async user config writer and scalar patch native facade.
entries.cannotShrinkFurtherBecause: CLI/admin callers import writeUserConfigFile/updateUserConfigStringScalar. Deletion requires migrating those public entrypoints to native/generated bindings.

entries.minimumTsRole: Tracked declaration surface for user-data-paths public API.
entries.cannotShrinkFurtherBecause: It is a published declaration artifact for a heavily imported runtime path API. Deletion requires replacing the declaration generation/public API surface, not config semantic migration.

entries.minimumTsRole: RCC subdir constants, native path resolver calls, and process.env publication for host runtime.
entries.cannotShrinkFurtherBecause: Many CLI/server/debug/runtime callers import this path API and ensureRccUserDirEnvironment mutates Node process.env. Deletion requires a separate runtime lifecycle/public path API migration.
